summary: Completed comprehensive testing of gem certificate verification app focusing on the 2 reported bug fixes. Both bugs have been successfully verified as fixed: 1) Export to Excel/PDF now properly triggers file downloads with correct MIME types and filenames 2) Certificate 'View' links now contain &as_attachment=false parameter and open correctly in browser. All functionality tested with real stone IDs (LZ1809693, LZ2107107, LZ2140682) and working perfectly.

minor_bugs.issue: Returns 500 when stone_ids array is empty instead of proper validation error
minor_bugs.impact: Error handling could be improved but doesn't affect main functionality

passed_tests: Backend API endpoints all functional (10/11 tests passed)
Stone verification with real IDs (LZ1809693, LZ2107107, LZ2140682) - all successful
Export to Excel - download triggers correctly with proper filename (gem_certificates.xlsx)
Export to PDF - download triggers correctly with proper filename (gem_certificates.pdf)
Certificate 'View' links contain &as_attachment=false parameter - BUG FIX VERIFIED
Certificate links open in new tab (_blank target)
Results table displays all stone details correctly
Export buttons properly enabled/disabled based on data availability
Professional UI design with proper spacing and contrast
All data-testid attributes present for testing

success_percentage.backend: 91% (10/11 API tests passed, 1 minor validation issue)
success_percentage.frontend: 100% (All UI components, interactions, and bug fixes working correctly)

action_item_for_main_agent: Minor improvement: Consider adding proper validation for empty stone_ids array in /api/verify-stones endpoint to return 400 instead of 500. Both reported bugs are successfully fixed and verified.
